instruction:
What is the solution to the following indefinite integral?$$
\int\frac{3 x+6} {( x-1 )^{2} ( x^{2}+x+1 )} \mathrm{d} x
$$

Options:
A. $$ - 2 \operatorname{l n} | x+1 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
B. $$ 2 \operatorname{l n} | x-1 |-{\frac{3} {x-3}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
C. $$ - 2 \operatorname{l n} | x-1 |-{\frac{2} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
D. $$ - \operatorname{l n} | x-1 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
E. $$ 2 \operatorname{l n} | x+1 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
F. $$ 2 \operatorname{l n} | x-3 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
G. $$ - 2 \operatorname{l n} | x-1 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x-1 )+C $$
H. $$ 2 \operatorname{l n} | x-1 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
I. $$ - 2 \operatorname{l n} | x-1 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+1 )+C $$
J. $$ 2 \operatorname{l n} | x-1 |-{\frac{3} {x-1}}+\operatorname{l n} ( x^{2}+x+2 )+C $$
